page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by 64 physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. Operating at 2.6 GHz with a dedicated single thread per core configuration, the hardware includes 256 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the system features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with Geekbench multi-core and software compilation tests scoring in the top 10% tier. Memory performance is also a key strength, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% and read-only large block memory bandwidth measured at 254.06 GB/sec. Single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are more moderate, with small-model text generation scoring in the bottom 10%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all rank in the top 25%.

The bare-metal architecture combined with energy-efficient ARM processors delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads. The inclusion of large local NVMe storage and high memory bandwidth makes this server type suitable for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and containerized microservices. It is less optimal for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or heavy LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, placing in the top 10% for software compilation and memory scores. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency, making it suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, and software development pipelines.

tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 platform combining sixty-four dedicated cores with high-speed DDR5 memory and local NVMe instance storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 Graviton cores running at 2.6 GHz
256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance and software compilation speeds
Strong database, Redis, and static web serving throughput
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for distributed workloads
